title: The Honeycomb.
content:
If thou hast found an honeycomb,
    Eat thou not all, but taste on some:
    For if thou eat'st it to excess,
    That sweetness turns to loathsomeness.
    Taste it to temper, then 'twill be
    Marrow and manna unto thee.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "The Honeycomb.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
